mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling over a recent 3-month window, discount-rate pressure on equities eases and valuation multiples expand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is falsified if rate cuts are not followed by better forward returns than unchanged-rate periods.
failure_condition: Rejected if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS in rolling 3-year samples, or if the sign of the relationship is negative in more than half of rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A high policy rate above 4.5% tightens financial conditions and raises the equity discount rate, so S&P 500 forward returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days while the policy stance remains restrictive. The effect weakens once the market fully prices future cuts.
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered observations with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% generate positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if triggered median returns are not below the unconditional median by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening household income growth and slower earnings momentum, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when the increase is broad rather than a one-month blip.
failure_condition: Rejected if UNRATE-rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances, or if the average triggered return is not significantly below zero across rolling out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: A two-month rise in RSAFS together with a high housing-start level indicates broad final-demand strength and supports cyclical revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails if sales improve while housing activity is simultaneously collapsing.
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint condition of rising RSAFS and elevated HOUST does not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 20-day SPX returns, or if interaction-only lift in hit rate is absent in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and better capacity utilization, which should lift cyclical earnings expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect is weaker when the rise is driven by price inflation rather than real output.
failure_condition: Rejected if positive 60-day SPX returns do not occur more often after INDPRO uptrends than after flat or declining INDPRO, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in CPIAUCSL below 3% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS stays above 5% implies real rates are high but inflation pressure is cooling, which should eventually support equity multiples and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails if disinflation is not accompanied by easier policy expectations.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation falls below 3% y/y while FEDFUNDS > 5%
failure_condition: Rejected if the triggered state is not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more often than the unconditional baseline, or if lower inflation with high FEDFUNDS fails to improve returns relative to high-FEDFUNDS episodes without disinflation.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle labor slowdown, a concurrent decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE should signal weakening consumption and margin pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The effect is concentrated when payroll softness is not offset by stronger production data.
failure_condition: Rejected if joint PAYEMS decline and UNRATE increase do not precede negative 5-day SPX returns more often than the unconditional rate, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value versus single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is weak while RSAFS also softens, aggregate demand is deteriorating and corporate revenue expectations should fall, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal is strongest in slowdowns that are not offset by labor-market resilience.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth weakens while RSAFS declines on a 3-month basis
failure_condition: Rejected if simultaneous weakness in GDPC1 and RSAFS does not predict negative 60-day SPX returns better than either variable alone, or if the sign flips positive in repeated slowdown episodes.
